completion_summary: Re-grounded PL-015-104 as non-Code commemorative history, PL-015-105, PL-015-106, PL-015-111, and PL-016-113 as active statutory-note enactments with concrete targets, PL-016-112 as superseded before codification, and PL-015-107, PL-015-108, PL-015-109, and PL-015-110 as unresolved because the supplied source bundle does not provide recoverable authenticated text.

laws.title: Public Law 15-104 | The Military Justice Reorganization and Reform Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: Current law, but it is commemorative and non-Code material rather than a general-and-permanent Code enactment. The reviewed source says no Code operation was authorized.
laws.exact_change: No Code insertion is authorized; keep the act only as non-Code public-law history.
source_evidence: codification/mass_migration/latest/reports/laws/PL-015-104.md classifies the enactment as active with disposition NON_CODE.
The same report says the law is commemorative rather than general and permanent U.S. Code material and authorizes no Code operation.
The prior review's title 10 placement is therefore overinclusive and should not be used as a permanent-text insertion.
laws.final_recommended_action: Remove the mistaken permanent-text treatment and retain PL-015-104 only as non-Code history.
laws.exact_enacted_change: No Code insertion is authorized; keep the act only as non-Code public-law history.

current_xml_comparison.detail: The current note declares "Repealed or superseded" and carries a full-text quoted-content dump plus a Trello link. The source is current, so the note is misclassified and should not be pasted into permanent text.

provision_level_conclusions: SEC 1-14 together present a broad military-justice reform package, but the review keeps the act out of permanent Code text and treats it as non-Code history.

laws.title: Public Law 15-105 | The Municipal Prosecution Act of 2023
laws.chronology_conclusion: Current enactment. The latest codification report treats the law as a statutory note at title 2, and the source text contains express repeal instructions rather than a wholesale positive-law rewrite.
laws.exact_change: Add a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 931 and record the act's express repeals and prosecution-assignment rules as note history, not as new positive-law text.
source_evidence: codification/mass_migration/latest/reports/laws/PL-015-105.md marks the law active, disposition STATUTORY_NOTE, with target /us/usc/t2/s931.
The source text includes express repeals of PL 12-88 Part D, PL 12-88 Title 6 section 37, and specified PL 15-97 subsections.
The reviewed primary batch had broader title 28 and 40 suggestions, but the latest codification report is more specific about the title 2 note placement.
laws.final_recommended_action: Use the title 2 statutory-note placement and keep the repeals as concise note history.
laws.exact_enacted_change: Add a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 931 and record the act's express repeals and prosecution-assignment rules as note history, not as new positive-law text.

current_xml_comparison.detail: The branch note is boilerplate, declares the law repealed or superseded, and fails to record the express repeals and amendments to PL 12-88 and PL 15-97. Placement at title 40 is odd; title 28 fits better. Trello link present; no dump.

laws.provision_level_conclusions: No recoverable provision-level conclusion can be drawn from the supplied source record.

laws.title: Public Law 15-111 | Presidential Transition Protection Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: Current enactment. It creates a continuing presidential-transition prohibition with defined terms and penalties, and the latest codification report places it as a title 2 statutory note.
laws.exact_change: Add a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 172 that captures the transition prohibition, definitions, and penalties.
source_evidence: codification/mass_migration/latest/reports/laws/PL-015-111.md marks the law active, disposition STATUTORY_NOTE, with target /us/usc/t2/s172.
The law text defines presidential transition terms, prohibits obstruction, and sets a short jail term and fine.
The title 49 placement in the original review is not the latest source-grounded anchor.
laws.final_recommended_action: Re-anchor the note to title 2 and keep the prohibition and penalty as a concise statutory note.
laws.exact_enacted_change: Add a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 172 that captures the transition prohibition, definitions, and penalties.

current_xml_comparison.detail: The note is placed under title 49, which is the wrong subject matter for a presidential-transition criminal-offense statute. Title 3 or title 18 fits better. The note is otherwise a boilerplate statutory note and includes a Trello link.

laws.provision_level_conclusions: No recoverable provision-level conclusion can be drawn from the supplied source record.

usc/usc02.xml contains the later USAR amendments note recording that Pub. L. 31-228 repealed PL-016-112 and reinstated the section in full.
audit/final-ledger.json records the review conclusion as superseded-before-codification with no operative codification surviving.
laws.final_recommended_action: Treat PL-016-112 as historical repeal material only and anchor the surviving text at 2 U.S.C. 192.
laws.exact_enacted_change: Reinstate 2 U.S.C. 192 in full and keep PL-016-112 only as repeal history.
